title: Fluentd Filter Plugin Drop Rate — Records Removed by rewrite/grep
dataSources: `index=fluent` `sourcetype=fluentd:plugins` filter plugins with `emit_count` in vs out, or `fluentd:log` drop counters
app: Fluentd 1.x filter metrics via monitor agent
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:plugins"
| where match(plugin_type, "^filter")
| eval dropped=coalesce(dropped_records, num_errors, filter_dropped)
| eval processed=coalesce(emit_records, emit_count, 1)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(dropped) as drops, sum(processed) as proc by _time, plugin_id, host
| eval drop_rate=round(100*drops/nullif(proc,0),3)
| eventstats median(drop_rate) as baseline by plugin_id
| where drop_rate > baseline * 3 AND drop_rate > 0.1
| sort -drop_rate
description: Aggressive `grep`, `record_transformer`, or sampling filters can silently discard security-relevant events after a bad regex deploy. Baseline drop_rate per filter to catch configuration mistakes.
value: Protects compliance-sensitive logs from accidental over-filtering.
implementation: If drops are not exposed, diff ingress totals pre/post filter using separate plugin metrics. Document allowed drop ceilings per filter in a lookup for static thresholds.
visualization: Area chart (drop_rate), table (plugin × host), annotation for config deploys.
grandmaExplanation: We read the forwarder and buffer logs for stress and give-ups so we see log loss risk before disks or patience run out.
knownFalsePositives: Buffer pain from downstream saturation, bad TLS, or local disk. We check agent and destination before blind buffer tuning.
